old_value: {"id":"broadside-goj2.35","title":"Signature does not require date in signed headers","description":"Similar to the digest issue: server.rs:657-674 checks the Date header freshness (within 5 minutes), but does not verify that 'date' is included in the Signature header's signed headers list. If an attacker can replay a captured request, they can replace the Date header (which was not signed) with a fresh timestamp, bypassing the replay protection entirely while the signature still verifies against the originally-signed headers. The fix is to reject requests where the Signature header's headers= parameter does not include both 'date' and 'digest'. This is an integrity issue: replay attacks on signed requests. Locations: server.rs:657-674 (Date freshness check), signatures.rs:62-63 (signed headers parsed but not validated for required fields).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@reviewcommit.com","created_at":"2026-07-18T17:30:15.075617278Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-18T17:30:15.075617278Z","labels":["crypto-security","untrusted-input","review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"broadside-goj2.35","depends_on_id":"broadside-goj2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-18T17:30:15.104118351Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

new_value: Inbox now requires date in signed headers list
